---
title: "Environment (micromamba)"
---

This workflow uses **micromamba** to create a reproducible environment with all required tools (Snakemake + aligners + utilities).

## Installing micromamba

micromamba can be installed from the [github repository](https://github.com/mamba-org/micromamba-releases).


```bash
"${SHELL}" <(curl -L https://micro.mamba.pm/install.sh)
```

## Create the workflow environment (project-local)

From the repo root:

```bash
# micromamba create -y -p .mamba/env -f env/environment.yml # save the env under the path ./mamba/env

micromamba create -y -n update_genomes -f env/environment.yml # save the env under the default path of miniconda3
```

To run commands inside the environment without activating it:

```bash
micromamba run -p .mamba/env snakemake --version
```

## Update the environment

If `env/environment.yml` changes (new tools, version bumps):

```bash
micromamba update -y -p .mamba/env -f env/environment.yml
```

## Health checks

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs env check
```

This step goes through the needed tools for the workflow and output if the tool versions. you should see something like that:

```
{
  "snakemake": "8.30.0",
  "STAR": "2.7.11b",
  "bwa": "",
  "bowtie2": "/fs/gpfs41/lv07/fileset07/home/b_cox/yeroslaviz/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes/bin/bowtie2-build-s version 2.5.5",
  "samtools": "samtools 1.23",
  "bamCoverage": "bamCoverage 3.x.x"
}
```

### Troubleshooting: missing `bamCoverage` / `deeptools`

If `./scripts/bcfngs env check` reports:

```text
Missing tools in environment: bamCoverage
```

use this sequence:

1. Update the environment from `env/environment.yml`:

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs env update
./scripts/bcfngs env check
```

2. If you use a non-default environment location, pass it explicitly:

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs --env-prefix /path/to/your/env env update
./scripts/bcfngs --env-prefix /path/to/your/env env check
```

3. Verify `deeptools` is actually installed in that environment:

```bash
micromamba list -p .mamba/env | grep -i deeptools
micromamba run -p .mamba/env bamCoverage --version
```

4. If still missing, recreate the environment:

```bash
rm -rf .mamba/env
./scripts/bcfngs env create
./scripts/bcfngs env check
```

`bamCoverage` is provided by the `deeptools` package in `env/environment.yml`.

## How-to (common scenarios)

### I’m new / first time setup

```bash
micromamba create -y -p .mamba/env -f env/environment.yml
./scripts/bcfngs env check
```

### Run a one-off command without activating

```bash
micromamba run -p .mamba/env snakemake --help
micromamba run -p .mamba/env STAR --version
```

### Keep tools up-to-date (routine)

1. Update the environment:

```bash
micromamba update -y -p .mamba/env -f env/environment.yml
./scripts/bcfngs env check
```

2. (Optional) record an explicit lockfile for reproducibility:

```bash
mkdir -p env/locks
micromamba list -p .mamba/env --explicit > env/locks/explicit-YYYY-MM-DD.txt
```

### I want to use HPC modules for vendor tools

You still need the micromamba env for `python` + `snakemake`, but aligners can come from modules:

```bash
module load bclconvert/4.3.13 star bowtie2 bwa
./scripts/bcfngs --profile modules env check
./scripts/bcfngs --profile modules genomes plan
./scripts/bcfngs --profile modules genomes build --cores 20
```

### Demultiplexing with bcl-convert (modules)

This repo doesn’t automate demultiplexing yet, but the typical pattern is:

```bash
module load bclconvert/4.3.13
bcl-convert --help

# Example placeholders (adjust to your run folder / SampleSheet):
# bcl-convert --bcl-input-directory /path/to/runfolder \
#            --output-directory /path/to/output \
#            --sample-sheet /path/to/SampleSheet.csv
```

## HPC modules fallback (optional)

If you prefer using HPC modules for vendor tools, you can load:

```bash
module load bclconvert/4.3.13 star bowtie2 bwa
```

You can still run Snakemake/python from micromamba (recommended).
